title: Marinated Crunchy Irish Corn Bowl
description: A fermented and crunchy Irish dish featuring creamed corn, made pescatarian-friendly and served in a Korean brass bowl. This adventurous recipe combines the flavors of Ireland with the presentation of Korea.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine corn kernels, smoked salmon, kimchi, and brine, and marinate for at least 2 hours in the refrigerator.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a separate saucepan, combine heavy cream and butter, and heat over medium heat until the butter is melted and the cream is warm.
Add the marinated corn mixture to the cream sauce and stir to combine.
Transfer the creamed corn mixture to a Korean brass bowl and top with chopped scallions and crispy garlic.
Sprinkle sesame seeds over the top of the dish for garnish.
Bake the dish in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned and the corn is heated through.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the Marinated Crunchy Irish Corn Bowl hot, garnished with additional scallions and sesame seeds if desired.
